fatty liver.base_explanation: Fatty liver means there is increased fat within the liver. This is a common finding and can be related to diet, weight, or metabolic factors.
fatty liver.follow_up_note: Doctors may correlate this with blood tests and lifestyle factors.

hepatomegaly.base_explanation: Hepatomegaly means the liver appears larger than normal on imaging.
hepatomegaly.follow_up_note: The cause varies and is interpreted together with clinical history and lab results.

splenomegaly.base_explanation: Splenomegaly refers to an enlarged spleen seen on imaging.
splenomegaly.follow_up_note: Doctors evaluate this finding in the context of blood tests and symptoms.

gallstones.base_explanation: Gallstones are solid deposits that form in the gallbladder. Many people have gallstones without symptoms.
gallstones.follow_up_note: Symptoms help determine whether this finding is clinically important.

gallbladder wall thickening.base_explanation: This finding means the wall of the gallbladder appears thicker than normal, which can occur for various reasons.
gallbladder wall thickening.follow_up_note: Clinical symptoms and blood tests help determine significance.

ascites.base_explanation: Ascites refers to the presence of fluid within the abdominal cavity.
ascites.follow_up_note: The cause and amount of fluid guide further evaluation.

bowel wall thickening.base_explanation: Bowel wall thickening means part of the intestine appears thicker than usual on imaging.
bowel wall thickening.follow_up_note: Doctors interpret this together with symptoms and lab findings.

dilated bowel loops.base_explanation: Dilated bowel loops indicate that sections of the intestine appear wider than normal.
dilated bowel loops.follow_up_note: This finding is evaluated alongside symptoms such as pain or vomiting.

liver cyst.base_explanation: A liver cyst is a fluid-filled sac within the liver. Most liver cysts are benign and incidental.
liver cyst.follow_up_note: Further evaluation is usually only needed if symptoms are present.

normal abdominal scan.base_explanation: This means no significant abnormalities were identified in the abdomen on imaging.
normal abdominal scan.follow_up_note: This is generally a reassuring result.
